murder.means: Dr. Celestine Moreau killed Lady Vivienne with the chapel's heavy brass candlestick.
murder.motive: Vivienne had discovered that Moreau had certified a beneficiary's death without examining the patient, allowing a charitable bequest to be released early. Vivienne intended to give the original certificate to Inspector Hayes after a private chapel meeting.
murder.opportunity: Moreau crossed the gallery at 21:38, entered the chapel before Vivienne's appointment, and had five unwitnessed minutes with her there. She returned to the hall before Captain Drake discovered the body.
murder.cover_story: Moreau says she stayed by the great-hall fire until Captain Drake raised the alarm and that Vivienne must have been alone in the chapel.

fact_moreau_identity.statement: Dr. Celestine Moreau murdered Lady Vivienne Ashford.

fact_certificate_forgery.statement: Moreau certified the death of an Ashwick beneficiary without examining the patient; the false certificate released a charitable bequest and exposed her to professional ruin.

fact_vivienne_intent.statement: Vivienne summoned Moreau to the chapel and meant to hand the original certificate to Inspector Hayes after the meeting.

fact_murder_method.statement: Vivienne died from one deliberate blunt blow to the temple.

fact_weapon.statement: The chapel's brass candlestick was used to deliver the fatal blow.

fact_murder_time.statement: The chapel clock and the body's condition place the fatal blow at about 21:45.

fact_moreau_gallery_crossing.statement: Captain Drake saw Moreau cross the rain-damp gallery toward the chapel at 21:38, carrying no medical bag.

fact_moreau_false_alibi.statement: Moreau's claim to have stayed in the great hall conflicts with both Drake's sighting and the wet gallery dust on her shoes.

fact_captain_threat.statement: Captain Drake threatened Vivienne over a naval memorial dispute, but he remained in the gallery and later found the body.

fact_edgar_dispute.statement: Edgar argued with Vivienne about a delayed property sale, but his library ledger places him there throughout the chapel murder window.

fact_kitchen_alibi.statement: Chef Dubois was repairing the kitchen stove during the murder window; the black grease on his cuff is not blood.

timeline.summary: Dinner ends; Vivienne asks Moreau to meet privately and tells Hayes to remain available.
Vivienne enters the chapel with an original certificate and waits beside the altar.
Captain Drake sees Moreau cross the wet gallery toward the chapel, without the medical bag she claims to have been fetching.
At 21:45 Moreau strikes Vivienne with the brass candlestick, hides the certificate in the vestry, and leaves the chapel.
Hayes notes the great-hall clock striking a quarter to ten while the house is otherwise quiet.
Captain Drake enters the chapel, finds Vivienne dead, and raises the alarm.
The survivors gather in the great hall as the storm cuts off the causeway.

lady_vivienne_ashford.private_motive: She intended to preserve the original certificate and expose the person who falsified it.
secrets: She arranged a private chapel meeting with Moreau and planned to trust Hayes with the document.

schedule.activity: Finishes dinner and prepares the certificate.
Waits at the altar for Moreau.

observations.summary: Vivienne places the original certificate beneath her prayer book, intending to give it to Hayes after confronting Moreau.
Moreau arrives and kills Vivienne with the brass candlestick.

lady_vivienne_ashford.alibi_claim: Not applicable; Lady Ashford is the victim.
goals: Leave enough of the truth behind to protect the estate.

relationships.private_summary: Vivienne had uncovered Moreau's false certificate.

dr_celestine_moreau.public_relationship_to_victim: A physician invited to advise the Ashwick charity.
dr_celestine_moreau.private_motive: The certificate would prove she violated her medical duty and profited from the early release of a bequest.
secrets: She killed Vivienne in the chapel and hid the original certificate in the vestry.

schedule.activity: Sits by the fire after dinner, watching Vivienne leave.
Crosses the damp gallery toward the chapel.
Confronts Vivienne, kills her, and conceals the certificate.
Returns to the hall and practices a calm alibi.

observations.summary: Moreau kills Vivienne with the brass candlestick and moves the certificate to the vestry.

dr_celestine_moreau.alibi_claim: I remained by the great-hall fire until Captain Drake called out from the chapel.
goals: Make the chapel meeting look like Vivienne's solitary retreat.

lies.claim: I did not leave the great hall before the alarm.
lies.reason: She must erase the short, fatal chapel appointment.

relationships.private_summary: Vivienne had become an immediate threat to her license and freedom.

inspector_elena_hayes.private_motive: She wants a clean account before the storm makes outside assistance possible.
secrets: Vivienne privately asked her to receive a document after a chapel meeting.

schedule.activity: Keeps the hall under quiet observation and notes the quarter-hour chime.

observations.summary: Hayes hears the hall clock strike a quarter to ten and sees that Moreau is not at the fire then.

inspector_elena_hayes.alibi_claim: I was in the great hall, noting the clock and waiting for Lady Ashford's promised document.
goals: Keep witnesses from contaminating the scene.

relationships.public_summary: A respectful guest of the household.
relationships.private_summary: Vivienne asked her to secure a troubling certificate.

captain_marcus_drake.public_relationship_to_victim: An old family friend with a public disagreement.
captain_marcus_drake.private_motive: He feared Vivienne would withdraw support from a naval memorial but did not intend violence.
secrets: He wrote an angry letter about the memorial and hid it in the gallery.

schedule.activity: Studies the family portraits and watches the passage to the chapel.
Finds the body and raises the alarm.

observations.summary: Drake sees Moreau cross toward the chapel, her shoes wet from the gallery window, and notices she carries no doctor’s bag.
Drake finds Vivienne beside the altar and calls for everyone to assemble.

captain_marcus_drake.alibi_claim: I was in the gallery until I checked the chapel and found Lady Ashford.
goals: Be believed about Moreau without making the old letter look like a confession.

relationships.private_summary: Their argument supplied a damaging but nonlethal motive.

chef_armand_dubois.private_motive: He worries a kitchen accident will be mistaken for evidence of violence.
secrets: He damaged the stove during service and was repairing it after dinner.

schedule.activity: Repairs the kitchen stove and keeps a greasy rag nearby.

observations.summary: Dubois is repairing the stove at the critical time; the dark stain on his cuff is grease.

chef_armand_dubois.alibi_claim: I was repairing the stove in the kitchen; the stain on me is black grease, nothing more.
goals: Clear the kitchen staff from suspicion.

relationships.private_summary: He respected her but feared losing his position over the stove.

sabrina_voss.private_motive: She wants to keep a private inheritance discussion out of the investigation.
secrets: Vivienne had promised to reconsider a small inheritance for Sabrina, but no papers were signed.

schedule.activity: Listens to the gramophone and writes an unsent letter.

observations.summary: At dinner Sabrina hears Vivienne ask Moreau for a private word before the guests disperse.

sabrina_voss.alibi_claim: I was in the drawing room with the gramophone after dinner.
goals: Avoid turning a private inheritance request into a motive for murder.

relationships.private_summary: Sabrina hoped for financial help but had no reason to harm Vivienne.

zara_okonkwo.private_motive: She wants to protect the charity records and not be blamed for them.
secrets: She had reviewed old beneficiary ledgers but never saw the original medical certificate.

schedule.activity: Reviews the estate ledgers and confirms Edgar's whereabouts.

observations.summary: Zara sees Edgar working through the property ledger during the chapel murder window.

zara_okonkwo.alibi_claim: I was in the study reviewing estate ledgers, and Edgar was there as well.
goals: Keep the charity records from being distorted into an accusation.

relationships.private_summary: Zara trusted Vivienne's concern about the charity.

edgar_blackwood.private_motive: A delayed property sale made him look obstructive, and he fears that disagreement will be treated as a motive.
secrets: He withheld a property ledger until he could correct an embarrassing arithmetic error.

schedule.activity: Reconciles the property ledger while Zara works nearby.

observations.summary: Edgar remains in the study ledger room during the chapel murder window.

edgar_blackwood.alibi_claim: Zara and I were in the study with the property ledger when the hall clock sounded.
goals: Avoid making the delayed sale look like a reason to kill Vivienne.

relationships.private_summary: Their property argument was real but not murderous.

ev_chapel_candlestick.name: Smeared brass candlestick
ev_chapel_candlestick.description: The altar candlestick bears a fresh wiped smear and a dent matching the wound.

ev_body_assessment.name: Temple wound assessment
ev_body_assessment.description: A single brass-edged blow and the warmth of the body fix a narrow time of death.

ev_vestry_certificate.name: Original beneficiary certificate
ev_vestry_certificate.description: The original certificate contains Moreau's certification and the beneficiary's unexamined death notation.

ev_vivienne_chapel_note.name: Vivienne's chapel note
ev_vivienne_chapel_note.description: A note asks Moreau to bring an explanation and says Hayes will receive the certificate after their meeting.

ev_gallery_mud.name: Rain-dark gallery dust
ev_gallery_mud.description: A distinctive wet heel trace crosses the gallery toward the chapel; its grit matches the mud on Moreau's shoes.

ev_hayes_clock_note.name: Hayes's clock note
ev_hayes_clock_note.description: Hayes's pocket notebook records the 21:45 hall chime and the empty chair by the fire.

ev_captain_sighting.name: Drake's gallery account
ev_captain_sighting.description: Drake's account places Moreau on the chapel route at 21:38.

ev_captain_letter.name: Angry memorial letter
ev_captain_letter.description: An unsent letter shows Drake's anger over the naval memorial, but not a route to the chapel at the murder time.
ev_captain_letter.red_herring_explanation: The letter establishes temper, not opportunity; Drake's own sighting and discovery account keep him in the gallery until after the killing.

ev_edgar_ledger.name: Property ledger
ev_edgar_ledger.description: The ledger records Edgar and Zara working in the study through the chapel murder window.

ev_kitchen_rag.name: Greasy stove rag
ev_kitchen_rag.description: The rag's stain is stove grease, consistent with Dubois's repair work.

opening.body_condition: Lady Ashford lies beside the chapel altar with a single temple wound; one candlestick is slightly out of line.
discoverer_observations: I found her at 22:00, beside the altar. The chapel was still and the candlestick looked recently handled.
opening.containment_statement: The causeway is flooded. Everyone remains inside Ashwick Manor until the truth is established.

initial_reactions.edgar_blackwood: Edgar insists the chapel was never part of his evening and asks for the estate papers to be secured.
initial_reactions.dr_celestine_moreau: Moreau offers clinical language about the wound, then says she had been in the great hall all evening.
initial_reactions.inspector_elena_hayes: Hayes asks everyone to preserve their exact recollection of the quarter-hour chime.
initial_reactions.chef_armand_dubois: Dubois worries that the grease on his cuffs will look terrible.
initial_reactions.sabrina_voss: Sabrina says Vivienne had asked Moreau for a private word after dinner.
initial_reactions.zara_okonkwo: Zara volunteers that Edgar was with her in the study.
